lines.text: Ms. Vess, I'll go straight to your founder. Mr. Hale hasn't exactly been shy about what he thinks of this program. [pause] Has he said any of that to you directly, inside the company.
[flatly] Frequently. In colorful terms. I can provide dates.
Then you'd grant me a man like that, with his hands still on the controls, is some kind of risk to a broadcast the whole country's going to be sitting down to watch.
He is not built for quiet. He posts what he thinks. [pause] Usually before breakfast. You have his feed. Subpoena his drafts folder if you want the rest.
There's a proposal in this room to hand Frontier to a federal receiver until Unity Day. For stability, they tell me. [pause] I expect you've got a view on that.
[deliberate] A receiver wouldn't know this company in six months. Your broadcast is in nine weeks. The Accord flies on schedule if the company stays in the hands that run it. Mine.
[flatly] Watch him as closely as you like. He's loud. His calendar's clean.
(close) [quietly] My COO and the mother of my children just...
— and when even Mira Vess won't defend him, what's left to defend?
